title: enrich: full-corpus A/B glossary vs no-glossary to validate dropping glossary field
description:
## Why

7-doc smoke (sample/baseline vs hybrid swap) showed no-glossary enrichment is ≥ baseline retrieval on 7/7 queries, with 1/7 strict improvement (Q4 \"signer details with INN tax id\" → ExtendedSignerDetails rose from #2 to #1, displacing generation.md).

Inline bilingual mentions in summary (\`Inn (tax identification number)\`, \`ТОРГ-12 (goods consignment note)\`) embed at least as well as YAML \`glossary: {Inn: ИНН ...}\` for cross-lingual retrieval. Cost savings: ~50% smaller LLM output, eliminates docf-5oj (glossary direction bug) at the source.

But n=7 is thin. Need full-corpus rerun before committing schema change.

## What

Decision experiment:

1. Re-enrich all 642 diadok docs with no-glossary prompt (probe at /tmp/enrich-no-glossary.mjs is the prototype — promote to a flag in docforge enrich)
2. Index + embed in dedicated qmd collection \`diadok-md-noglossary-642\` (vectors auto-deduped if hashes overlap with diadok-md, but content differs so expect 2600+ new embeddings)
3. Run the full 8-query original retrieval smoke (Q1-Q8 from 2026-05-10 session) PLUS the 7 targeted queries (Q9-Q15 from this session) with rerank ENABLED (not --no-rerank)
4. Score per-query: top-1 doc match (binary), top-3 doc match (binary), MRR contribution
5. Aggregate: % queries where no-glossary top-1 = baseline top-1 (no-regression rate); % queries where no-glossary top-1 better; % worse

## Acceptance criteria for dropping glossary

- No-regression rate ≥ 90% (out of ~15 queries, ≤ 1 may regress)
- No-glossary improvement count ≥ baseline-improvement count
- Wall-clock per doc: no-glossary ≤ baseline (sanity; should be faster)
- Eyeball spot-check: bilingual term coverage in 5 random no-glossary summaries → all major Russian terms have EN equivalents in parens

## Decision

If criteria met → proceed to schema-v2 bead (drop glossary field, depend on this).
If not met → keep glossary, address docf-5oj separately.

## Notes

- Use 9B model for both (fair comparison; 4B already shown worse in earlier session)
- Concurrency=1, parallel=1 for stable timing
- Embedding model: qwen3-embedding-8b (already canonical via QMD_EMBED_MODEL env, just mislabeled in DB metadata)
- Time budget: ~15 min enrich + 5 min embed + 5 min query = 25 min total
notes:
VERDICT 2026-05-11: gold-judged scoring PASS. 3-way A/B against manually defined gold (B/N/T):
- top1@gold: baseline 10/15, nogloss 13/15 (winner), tight 8/15
- top3@gold: baseline 13/15, nogloss 15/15 (perfect), tight 11/15
- MRR: 0.789 / 0.933 / 0.677

NoGloss vs baseline pairwise: 4 wins, 10 ties, 1 regression (Q7 'electronic signature concept' → picks glossary/index.md instead of glossary/signature.md; signature.md still in top-3).

Spec acceptance criteria PASS:
- no-regression rate 14/15 = 93.3% > 90%
- improvement count (4) > regression count (1)

Tight prompt (extra 'no enumeration' instructions) HURT — suppressed useful inline bilingual mentions. 8/15 worst score.

Closing PASS. Proceed to docf-eew (schema v2 ship).

Artifacts:
- score script: /tmp/score-gold.mjs
- raw traces: /tmp/score-gold.json
- prior 3-way (file-match): /tmp/score-3way.tsv
- enrich runners: /tmp/enrich-no-glossary-642.mjs (nogloss), /tmp/enrich-tight-642.mjs (tight)
- collections: diadok-md-noglossary-642 (kept for posterity), diadok-md-tight-642 (kept)
close_reason: Closed

title: docforge: 4-way A/B add raw-no-enrich arm
description: Run 4-way retrieval A/B baseline/nogloss/tight/raw vs gold to decompose translation contribution from multilingual vec alone. Setup: ingest ~/docs/diadok-md (raw) as new qmd collection diadok-md-raw-642 then score all 4 against 15-query gold. Caveat: 15-query gold post-hoc biased; rigorous follow-up = docf-73t.
notes:
4-way gold A/B (15 queries, post-hoc gold). RUN 2026-05-11.

Collections scored:
- baseline (qmd diadok-md → ~/docs/diadok-md-enriched, v1 glossary+EN)
- nogloss  (diadok-md-noglossary-642, v2 EN summary, schema v2)
- tight    (diadok-md-tight-642, terse EN no-enumeration)
- raw      (diadok-md-raw-642, ~/docs/diadok-md, NO enrichment, RU body only)

Results:
collection   top1@gold  top3@gold  MRR    miss
baseline     10/15      12/15      0.767  1
nogloss      12/15      15/15      0.900  0
tight         9/15      10/15      0.683  1
raw          12/15      14/15      0.880  0

KEY FINDING: raw (no enrichment, pure RU + Qwen3-Embedding-8B-Q4 multilingual vec) TIES nogloss on top1@gold. Translation contribution = +1 top3 + 0.020 MRR. Within 15-query noise floor.

Surprise: Q4+Q12 (signer details with INN) — only raw scores top1. RU body "ИНН" repeats outweigh single EN mention; multilingual vec bridges cross-lingual gap.

Q9 (deal documentation flow) — only EN-enriched arms win. So EN summary DOES help some queries.

Q7 (electronic signature) — flipped vs prior 3-way run (baseline+raw G, nogloss+tight X). Reranker + LLM-expansion non-determinism. Same gold definition.

CAVEATS:
- 15 queries, post-hoc, single observer (confirmation-bias risk)
- All queries EN; RU-query mix likely widens raw's lead
- Reranker variance ~1 query flip between runs

INTERPRETATION:
- Multilingual vec handles cross-lingual retrieval respectably on its own
- qmd BM25 layer benefits from EN tokens but RU body already has rich technical-term overlap (URLs, code identifiers, schema names) — diminishing return on translation
- Translation gain is real but small; not statistically defensible on 15 queries
- Need docf-73t (rigorous gold) to settle

NEXT: docf-73t — multi-doc hand-authored gold, blind authoring, larger N (50+), include RU-language queries.
close_reason: 4-way A/B complete. Raw arm proves multilingual vec handles RU→EN solo respectably; v2 enrich gain ~marginal on 15-query gold. Results captured in notes + memory docf-mt4-4-way-a-b-2026-05. Rigorous settlement awaits docf-73t.

title: enrich: drop glossary field from EnrichmentResponse, ship schema v2 (no-glossary, bilingual-summary-only)
description:
## Why

Pending validation by the full-corpus A/B experiment. If experiment confirms no-regression, glossary is removable: inline bilingual term mentions in summary cover the same retrieval surface with less hallucination, less LLM output cost, and zero glossary-direction inconsistency (docf-5oj).

## What

Schema change:

\`\`\`typescript
// src/enrich/types.ts
export interface EnrichmentResponse {
  title_en: string;
  summary_en: string;
  // glossary: REMOVED in v2
}

export const ENRICH_JSON_SCHEMA = {
  type: 'object',
  additionalProperties: false,
  required: ['title_en', 'summary_en'],
  properties: {
    title_en: { type: 'string', minLength: 1 },
    summary_en: { type: 'string', minLength: 1 },
  },
};

export const ENRICH_SCHEMA_VERSION = 2;
\`\`\`

Prompt change (src/enrich/prompt.ts):

- Drop rule 3 (glossary)
- Strengthen rule 2 (summary): \"3-4 sentences. CRITICAL: mention each major Russian acronym, regulatory ID, and key technical term ONCE on first occurrence with its English equivalent in parentheses, e.g. 'ТОРГ-12 (goods consignment note)', 'ЭДО (electronic document interchange / EDI)'. This serves as the only English-Russian bridge — no separate glossary will be emitted.\"
- Drop seed-glossary injection (no longer needed)
- Drop aggregator (GlossaryAggregator becomes dead code, remove)

Output change (src/enrich/output.ts):

- Drop \`glossary\` from frontmatter
- Drop \`> Keys:\` line from blockquote (only \`> EN:\` remains)
- Bump \`enrich_schema_version\` to 2

Pipeline change (src/enrich/pipeline.ts):

- Remove GlossaryAggregator, glossary seed loading, aggregator.absorb calls
- Cache invalidation: schema_version bump → all v1 cache entries become invalid (already keyed on enrich_hash which incorporates schema_version)

CLI change:

- Drop \`--glossary-seed\` flag from enrich subcommand
- Update --help

Tests:

- Update llm-client.test.ts: remove glossary assertions
- Update pipeline.test.ts (if any glossary path exists): remove
- Update output.test.ts: assert no \`glossary:\` in frontmatter, no \`> Keys:\` line
- Update eval/runner.test.ts: glossary metric becomes vestigial — either drop from runner or keep as legacy/zero
- Update gold fixture (tests/enrich/fixtures/gold/reference/torg12.json): remove glossary field
- Add new golden output fixtures for v2 schema

Migration:

- Existing diadok-md-enriched dir is v1. Re-run \`docforge enrich --force\` to produce v2.
- Old enrich_hash cache entries invalidated automatically (schema_version is in hash input)
- Document migration in CHANGELOG (if exists) or README

## Closes / supersedes

- docf-5oj (glossary direction inconsistency) — moot when glossary removed
- Any future docf-* glossary-related bugs

## Depends on

- Decision experiment bead (full-corpus A/B). Don't ship until that bead closes with positive result.
close_reason: closed by docf-eew commit 993ea31 (schema v2 ships; glossary removed)

title: enrich: inject per-section preamble for big docs to lift chunk-level EN retrieval
description:
## Why

qmd embeds each chunk as \`title + chunk_text\` (verified: store.ts:1524 + llm.ts:51). Only doc TITLE is prepended; summary_en + glossary live ONLY in YAML frontmatter at top of file → only chunk 0 carries them.

Big docs (utd.md = 82K → ~30 chunks) end up with 1 EN-anchored chunk + 29 bare-Russian chunks. EN semantic queries match the title-only signal weakly → big docs surface but lose to atomic schema docs (e.g. Q11 \"parse buyer title received document\" → utd.md ranked #2 @ 56% vs ParseUniversalCorrectionDocumentBuyerTitleXml.md @ 93%).

qmd has no chunk-prefix injection knob. \`addContext\` is display-only (returned in search-result \`context\` field, never embedded — checked store.ts:3573/3718 + embed loop at 1524).

## qmd chunking algorithm (per upstream README)

- Target ~900 tokens, scan 200-token window before cutoff
- Break-point scores: H1=100, H2=90, H3=80, H4=70, code-fence=80, hr=60, blank=20, list=5
- Decay: \`score × (1 - (distance/window)² × 0.7)\` — squared distance penalty
- Code fences NOT cut points (kept whole)
- Markdown uses regex chunking only (AST chunking is .ts/.js/.py/.go/.rs only)

Implication: qmd cuts AT \`## \` boundaries (score 90, dominates). Each chunk STARTS with \`## Section\` heading.

## What

Post-enrich pass over enriched .md output:
1. Detect docs above threshold (proposal: > 4K tokens, ~50 of 642 diadok docs)
2. Parse frontmatter (title_en, summary_en, glossary)
3. Walk markdown; **immediately AFTER each \`## \` and \`### \` heading**, inject a compact preamble block:
   \`\`\`
   ## Section Title
   
   <!-- doc-anchor -->
   > **Doc:** {title_en}. {summary_en truncated ~200 chars}
   > **Keys:** k1=v1; k2=v2; ... (top-N glossary entries by source-frequency)
   
   [section body]
   \`\`\`
4. Skip H4+ (anchor spam)
5. Emit rewritten .md to same output path (overwrite)

## Placement rationale

qmd cuts AT the H2 boundary → anchor MUST go AFTER the heading so it lands at the START of the new chunk (not end of previous).

WRONG: \`> anchor\n## Section\` — anchor stays in prior chunk.
RIGHT: \`## Section\n> anchor\n[body]\` — anchor enters new chunk's first ~150 tokens.

## Acceptance

- Module \`src/enrich/section-anchor.ts\` + tests
- CLI flag \`--anchor-sections\` on \`docforge enrich\` (off by default; opt-in until validated)
- Unit test: utd-shaped fixture with 5 H2 + 3 H3 sections → output has 8 anchor blocks each placed AFTER the corresponding heading
- Unit test: small doc (< threshold) → no rewrite, byte-identical
- Unit test: doc with H4+ headings → H4+ skipped, no anchor injected
- A/B retrieval smoke: rerun 8-query smoke before/after \`--anchor-sections\` flag — utd.md should rank ≥ #1 on at least 1 of (Q9 seller title, Q10 amendment, Q11 parse buyer title)
- Threshold + top-N glossary truncation are config flags

## Open

- Glossary truncation strategy: top-N by source frequency vs all (latter inflates chunks). Start with N=8.
- Anchor format: blockquote (\`> Doc:\`) vs HTML comment (\`<!-- Doc: ... -->\`) — blockquote IS visible in rendered MD but doesn't disrupt downstream tools. Comment is invisible but qmd may strip in future. Lean blockquote.
- Mid-section overflow chunks (sections > 900 tokens split into 2+ chunks): chunks beyond first lose anchor. Accepted trade-off — most diadok sections are <900 tokens. Revisit if measurable.

title: enrich: investigate context-overflow on small input docs
description: 11 docs failed with HTTP 400 'Context size has been exceeded' on diadok run 2026-05-10 even at 32K LM Studio context. Most are huge inputs (ReleaseNotes 268K, GetContent 263K) but orderrequest.md is only 6.4K (~2K tokens). Hypothesis: JSON schema strict mode (llama.cpp grammar) causes unbounded generation loop on certain Russian formal-doc patterns. Repro: run enrich on diadok-md-fresh corpus with qwen3.5-9b @ 32768 context. Fix candidates: (a) add max_tokens cap on completion request, (b) limit glossary to N keys via schema.maxProperties, (c) bump LM Studio to 96K context for outliers, (d) add --max-bytes flag to enrich CLI to skip huge inputs.
close_reason: Root cause: docforge omitted max_tokens, causing llama.cpp to reserve prompt+remaining_context as n_keep. Under parallel sharding (n_ctx/parallel), reservation overflowed even for small inputs. Fix in commit on master adds max_tokens=2048. Live diadok run: 8/8 Type-B failures resolved at parallel=1 even before fix; with fix, parallel=4 should also work. 3 Type-A failures (true input-overflow on huge docs) need either 96K context or smart truncation -- separate issue if pursued.

title: [docforge] test kreuzberg-py binding vs html-to-markdown lib
description:
Mirror the docforge_node v0.2 experiment in Python: swap docforge's html_to_markdown.convert call for kreuzberg.extract_bytes_sync with output_format='markdown'. Keep BS4 preprocessing (body-picker + noise-strip + Pygments flatten). Run on diadok 642-file corpus, diff body-MD vs current docforge output.

Hypothesis: Same 98.3% parity + perf delta as Node port. If true, docforge could drop html-to-markdown dep (no longer maintained as separate kreuzberg-dev release? — verify) and stand on the kreuzberg core directly, with same plugin/eval ecosystem access as the rest of kreuzberg.

Acceptance:
- new branch + venv install kreuzberg python package
- swap convert.py:_convert call to kreuzberg.extract_bytes_sync(cleaned_body_html, 'text/html', config=ExtractionConfig(output_format='markdown'))
- run pytest tests/ — record golden delta
- run convert against ~/docs/diadok, diff body-MD vs current /tmp/diadok-py output
- report: % match, perf, dep size

Reference: bd memory docforge-node-v0-2-diadok-dogfood for the Node version's findings + the outputFormat='markdown' gotcha (default is Plain — drops headings/lists/fences).
close_reason: kreuzberg engine swap landed. Spec docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-09-docforge-kreuzberg-rewrite-design.md; plan docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-09-docforge-kreuzberg-rewrite.md. Goldens regenerated (only sphinx-method diffed; 5 of 6 already match Kreuzberg output). Full corpus 642/642, --version stamps 0.3.0, all 8 acceptance gates passed (incl. uv tool install reinstalled local docforge to 0.3.0).

title: enrich: register per-file EN context with qmd via 'qmd context add' for search-result UX
description:
## Why

qmd's \`addContext(collectionName, pathPrefix, contextText)\` API stores per-path context that is RETURNED in search results (in the \`context\` field of get/multi-get/query results — verified store.ts:3573/3718/4193/4267).

This context is NOT embedded into vectors (so it does NOT help retrieval — that's owned by docf-1fi's anchor injection). But it IS surfaced to AI consumers reading qmd search output, so it improves the *interpretability* of every hit.

Currently the consumer only sees: filepath, title, body snippet (in Russian). Adding EN context means every hit ships with an EN summary the consumer can reason about without parsing frontmatter from the body.

## What

After \`docforge enrich\` completes, walk the enriched output dir and call qmd CLI to register per-file context:

\`\`\`
qmd context add diadok-md <relpath> "{title_en}. {summary_en}\nKeys: k1=v1; k2=v2; ..."
\`\`\`

Or programmatically via qmd's \`addContext\` API (if we depend on qmd as a library).

## Acceptance

- New CLI flag on \`docforge enrich\`: \`--register-qmd-context <collection>\` (off by default)
- When set, post-enrich step shells out to \`qmd context add <collection> <relpath> "<text>"\` for each enriched file
- Context text format: \`{title_en}. {summary_en truncated to 300 chars}. Keys: {top-8 glossary entries}\`
- Skip files where context already matches (idempotent)
- Smoke: enrich gold fixture with flag, run \`qmd context list <collection>\` → entry visible
- Smoke: \`qmd query "consignment note" --collection diadok-md\` → result \`context\` field contains the registered EN summary

## Notes

- Cheap (~30 LOC + tests). Independent of docf-1fi.
- Stacks well with docf-1fi: anchor injection lifts retrieval (vector-side); context add lifts result interpretability (display-side).
- Failure mode: if qmd CLI not on PATH, log warning, skip. Don't fail the enrich run.

title: enrich: model produces reversed/identity glossary entries (EN as key, RU as value)
description: Aggregated glossary from diadok run 2026-05-10 has 2079 entries with quality issues: (1) some keys are EN with RU value (e.g. '1C module: 1С модуль', 'acceptance certificate: акт о выполнении работ / оказании услуг'); (2) some are identity (e.g. '200 OK: 200 OK', '401 Unauthorized: 401 Unauthorized'); (3) some are definitional not translational (e.g. '402 Payment Required: API subscription expired for the specified box'). Prompt should enforce: key MUST be Russian (Cyrillic) source term; value MUST be English translation; skip identity mappings; never include error-code descriptions. Fix: tighten BASE_SYSTEM in src/enrich/prompt.ts with explicit examples of allowed/disallowed glossary entries. Add a JSON schema constraint or post-validator that drops entries where the key is pure ASCII.
notes: DEFERRED 2026-05-11: glossary direction bug may be moot if docf-slw confirms no-glossary is viable. If docf-slw → docf-eew (drop glossary), this issue becomes superseded. Revisit only if docf-eew is rejected.
close_reason: closed by docf-eew commit 993ea31 (schema v2 ships; glossary removed)

title: Rebuild docforge enrich gold corpus with multi-doc hand-authored pairs
description: Current gold has 1 stub fixture that was hand-fixed during smoke. For real eval coverage need 5-10 hand-authored RU source/reference pairs spanning protocol docs, REST endpoints, and proto messages. Each reference should reflect realistic enrich output shape (RU->EN glossary, summary mentioning Diadoc-specific terms, must_preserve only listing tokens present in source).

title: enrich: warn on empty-string env var interpolation in config
description:
src/enrich/config.ts:96 interpolateEnv replaces unset vars with ''. For api_key that may be intentional (no auth), but for endpoint/model an unset var silently produces broken values like 'http:///v1'. Add a warn log when interpolation yields empty for required fields.
Source: final code review of d4916e8

title: enrich: extract expandHome to shared util + remove unused buildUserMessage
description:
Final review of M6 found two HIGH cleanup items:
1) expandHome duplicated across 4 files (src/cli.ts uses process.env.HOME chain, src/enrich/cli.ts + src/enrich/eval/cli.ts + src/openapi/cli.ts use os.homedir()). Extract to src/utils/path.ts.
2) buildUserMessage in src/enrich/prompt.ts exported but never called by pipeline/cli/runner. Either wire it through callLlm userMessage construction or remove.
Source: final code review of d4916e8

title: [docforge] Structured JSON report on stdout
description: Add --report-json flag. Emit {converted, failed, skipped, files: [...]} on stdout, logs still on stderr. Trigger: piping into CI/reporting tools.

title: [docforge] Incremental conversion (mtime/hash skip)
description: Skip files where output mtime is newer than input. Optional content hash check. Trigger: corpora grow large, full reconvert too slow.

title: [docforge] Configurable enrichment story
description: Per-source rules to add tags/category/version/lang to output frontmatter or Source-line tags. .docforge.yml in source root. Trigger: downstream consumer needs these fields.

title: [docforge] Plugin registry seam (Option C)
description: Define backend interface, ship Kreuzberg+Docling+native by default. Anyone adds backend via entry-point. Trigger: 2nd backend competes for same format (e.g. MinerU vs Docling for PDFs).

title: [docforge] Add concurrency --jobs N
description: Parallel file conversion via multiprocessing or threading. Only needed if real corpus exceeds 10s single-threaded. Trigger: corpus growing past 5000 files.

title: [docforge] Add --selector CLI override + broader selector chain
description: --selector flag accepts CSS selector. Default chain extends with main, article, .content. Handles MkDocs/Hugo/generic HTML. Trigger: non-Sphinx custom themes.

title: [docforge] Add URL fetch input
description: Support docforge https://docs.foo.com/page --output ./out. Single page in v2, recursive crawl deferred. Add httpx dep. Trigger: downloading docs from web routinely.

title: [docforge] Add OpenAPI/Swagger adapter
description: Parse OpenAPI YAML/JSON, emit one MD per endpoint with method, path, params, responses. Trigger: ingesting API spec.
close_reason: Shipped in docforge 0.2.0. `docforge openapi <spec> --output <dir>` emits per-endpoint + per-schema MD with relative $ref links. Validated end-to-end against diadoc spec (125 endpoints + 483 schemas in <1s, qmd ingest + cross-lingual smoke query passing).

title: [docforge] Add MD passthrough adapter
description: Adapter for existing MD trees: read file, prepend # title + Source line, write to output. No conversion. Trigger: ingesting existing MD docs (READMEs, MkDocs source).
close_reason: Marginal value. qmd ingests MD natively; header rewrite mostly cosmetic since qmd metadata already exposes path. Reopen if reranker proves to need inline path provenance.

title: [docforge] Add code-repo walker adapter
description: Walk git tree, classify files by extension, concat with breadcrumbs. Output one MD per file or one MD per dir. Trigger: ingesting code repo as searchable docs.
close_reason: Superseded by qmd --chunk-strategy auto (native AST chunking for code files). Walking repo → MD wrappers loses AST signal vs qmd ingesting source directly with glob mask.

title: [docforge] Add Office (DOCX/PPTX/XLSX) backend via Docling
description: Add Docling backend for Office formats. Router dispatches DOCX/PPTX/XLSX to Docling, HTML stays on Kreuzberg. Trigger: first time user needs to ingest Word/PowerPoint.

title: enrich: handle 'skipped' status in CLI tally + threshold denominator
description:
src/enrich/types.ts EnrichStatus declares 'skipped' but src/enrich/cli.ts only counts ok/cached/failed. If 'skipped' is ever emitted it drops silently. Either remove from union or add tally + threshold-denominator handling.
Source: final code review of d4916e8

value: docforge_go v0.1 thin Go port (commit 41343b2 in ~/experiements/docforge_go/) using kreuzberg-go v5 ExtractBytesSync. 8/9 fixtures convert; sphinx-method crashes JSON unmarshal — DjotLink.Attributes is *string in Go binding (binding.go:5243) but Rust emits array when <a class='...'> present. Auto-gen by alef. Quality vs Python docforge: nested list preserved, blockquote bullets preserved, BUT loses # H1 (renders as indented text), loses top-level '- ' list dashes, emits duplicate flat trailer of nested-list content. To reach parity: body-picker + noise-strip via goquery before feeding to kreuzberg. Build: cargo build -p kreuzberg-ffi --release in ~/experiements/kreuzberg + make build. Runtime: needs LD_LIBRARY_PATH=~/experiements/kreuzberg/target/release.

Docling 2.93.0 (HEAD) drops nested list children when Sphinx wraps sublist in <blockquote><div> (real diadok shape). Root: html_backend.py:2310/2349/2372 use 'recursive=False' on <li>{ul,ol} sublist search, missing blockquoted children. Synthetic <li><ul> direct-child fixture does NOT reproduce; need blockquote-wrapped fixture. Kreuzberg+BS4 (docforge) preserves all 14 sub-fields as '> *' blockquoted bullets.

kreuzberg-py 4.9 ExtractionConfig(use_cache=False, output_format='markdown') matches docforge needs. Configuration audit (brainstorm 2026-05-09) — strip_tags/preprocessing.preset/content_filter/extract_metadata are no-ops for our pipeline because BS4 already strips chrome upstream; only the two flags above matter. 5 of 6 sphinx fixtures (sphinx-proto, sphinx-proto-blockquote, sphinx-guide, sphinx-internal-link, sphinx-highlight-default) match Kreuzberg output as-authored vs old html-to-markdown — only sphinx-method needed regen (language-http tag drop). Lost code-block language tags is intentional and matches prototype quality bar.

kreuzberg Python (kreuzberg-py 4.9) and Node (@kreuzberg/node 4.x) bindings produce byte-identical Markdown for HTML input on docforge sphinx fixtures (sphinx-method, sphinx-proto, sphinx-proto-blockquote, sphinx-guide, sphinx-internal-link, sphinx-highlight-default — 6 of 6 byte-match). Goldens captured against Python binding pass unchanged against Node binding when both call extractBytesSync with outputFormat='markdown' useCache=false. Cross-binding regen via scripts/regen-goldens.ts not needed for HTML-only paths. Discovered docforge ts-rewrite Wave 2 2026-05-10 commit 575f7ee.

docforge beads moved to dedicated DB beads_docforge on 192.168.1.34:3307 with prefix docf- (suffixes preserved: infra-9tm -> docf-9tm etc). 25 issues + 12 memories migrated 2026-05-10 from shared beads DB. Stubs in shared beads closed with close_reason pointing here. Local .beads/ now points at beads_docforge. Backup .beads.bak.* created in repo root (gitignored). Other docforge sibling repos (docforge_go, docforge_node) NOT yet migrated — still use shared beads if they have .beads at all.

docforge_node v0.2 (sibling repo ~/experiements/docforge_node/, head commit on main) achieves 98.3% body-MD exact match (631/642 files) vs Python docforge on diadok corpus. Pipeline: cheerio body-picker + Sphinx noise strip + Pygments flatten -> @kreuzberg/node extractBytesSync({outputFormat:'markdown'}) -> rewriteInternalLinks. 1.6x faster (2.6s vs 4.2s). 11 diffs are kreuzberg-node renderer quirks: deep <blockquote> -> '## > > *' heading; trailing <hr> dropped. Node finds 2 extra files Python skips (genindex.html, search.html — Sphinx auto-gen indexes). KEY DISCOVERY: must pass {outputFormat:'markdown'} to extractBytesSync; default is Plain (drops headings/lists/fences/numbers). Without that, output looks broken. Same probably true for kreuzberg-go.

docf-mt4 4-way A/B 2026-05-11: raw (no enrichment, pure RU body + Qwen3-Embedding-8B-Q4 multilingual vec) tied with v2 nogloss on top1@gold (12/15 both) on 15-query post-hoc gold. v1 baseline 10/15, tight 9/15. nogloss gain over raw = +1 top3 + 0.020 MRR — within noise floor of 15-query gold. Translation step contributes real but marginal lift; qmd's BM25 layer benefits less than theory predicts because RU body already has rich code/URL/schema-name overlap with EN queries. Multilingual vec layer handles cross-lingual retrieval respectably solo. Raw beat all enriched arms on Q4+Q12 (signer/INN) because RU 'ИНН' token repeats dominate single EN mention. Decision on whether to drop enrich entirely awaits docf-73t real gold (blind-authored, 50+ queries, RU+EN mix). 15-query test cannot settle.

docforge HTML backend choice: Kreuzberg html-to-markdown v3.3 BEATS Docling on Sphinx HTML. Empirical: Docling drops nested list children (issue #2330 confirmed) — diadok Address.html test, Kreuzberg keeps all 12 sub-fields, Docling drops them. Use BS4 [itemprop=articleBody] selector first, fallback to div[role=main] then articleBody-inside, else None=skip (no raw soup fallback). Pre-flatten Pygments div.highlight->pre><code class=language-X>.

docforge prototype that proved the design: /tmp/diadok-conv/convert_kreuzberg.py + rewrite_links.py + .venv/. Already converted 642 Sphinx HTML files (Russian Diadoc API docs) at ~/docs/diadok to ~/docs/diadok-md in 5.8s single-threaded. Generalizing this proven script into reusable engine. v2 will add Office (Docling), URL fetch, --selector override, plugin registry — all tracked as infra-* issues with [docforge] prefix.

docforge rewrite to TypeScript landed 2026-05-10 on branch ts-rewrite (20+ commits). Stack: Node 20 + npm + ESM, cheerio + @kreuzberg/node + commander + js-yaml + vitest + tsx. Python codebase removed (15 src files + 15 test files + pyproject.toml + uv.lock). Wave 5 dogfood: convert produced 642 .md files vs ~/docs/diadok-md (only diff = intentional YAML frontmatter -> Context7 inline header swap + trailing newline); openapi produced 608 files (125 endpoints + 483 schemas) matching published diadoc-openapi qmd collection. 121 TS tests across 13 files. Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-09-docforge-typescript-rewrite-design.md. Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-09-docforge-typescript-rewrite.md. Dogfood report: docs/superpowers/dogfood-2026-05-09-ts.md. SUPERSEDES docforge-v1-ships-dogfooded-against-diadok-2026-05 (Python v1) and docforge-project-standalone-python-cli-converting-documentat.

docforge v1 ships: dogfooded against diadok 2026-05-08. 642 files converted in 5.6s, body-content diff vs prior Kreuzberg pipeline: 0 text lines differ (7427 diff lines are all structural improvements: language-annotated code fences, preserved <hr> separators, no trailing whitespace). Header format intentionally changed from YAML frontmatter to Context7 inline. Repo at ~/experiements/docforge, install via 'uv tool install git+file:///home/igi21/experiements/docforge'.

docf-slw closed PASS via gold-judged A/B (15 queries, manual gold sets). NoGloss schema v2 beats baseline 13/15 vs 10/15 top1@gold and 0.933 vs 0.789 MRR. Single regression Q7 (glossary/index over glossary/signature; signature still top-3). Tight prompt (extra 'no enumeration' instructions) hurt — 8/15. Lesson: inline bilingual term mentions in summary_en provide the cross-lingual retrieval bridge that the glossary YAML + Keys: body line provided in v1. Removing glossary YAML AND keeping rich summary text wins. Suppressing those mentions (tight) loses. Schema simplification + retrieval gain (cleaner top-1) + cost reduction (no glossary tokens) all aligned.

docforge implementation plan ready for execution at docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-08-docforge-implementation.md (1100 lines, 5 waves, 22 tasks, ~90 test assertions). Architect reviewed + 2 blocking gaps fixed (autolink form coverage in links.py + sphinx-internal-link integration test). Beads chain: infra-5hn (W0 scaffold) -> infra-sk0 (W1 pure helpers) AND infra-zc3 (W2 convert+goldens, parallel) -> infra-a1b (W3 cli+e2e) -> infra-29g (W4 dogfood diadok). Execution mode: subagent-driven-development (recommended) via superpowers skill — fresh subagent per task, two-stage review.

docforge project: standalone Python CLI converting documentation HTML to MD for QMD ingestion. Repo at ~/experiements/docforge. Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-08-docforge-design.md. v1 = HTML only via Kreuzberg + BS4 [itemprop=articleBody] selector. Wave issues: infra-5hn (W0 scaffold) → infra-sk0 (W1 helpers) → infra-zc3 (W2 convert+goldens) → infra-a1b (W3 CLI+e2e) → infra-29g (W4 dogfood). All prefixed [docforge] in title.

QMD does NOT strip YAML frontmatter — verified via lex query for unique frontmatter string in zod corpus (matched score 1.0 on lines 3-7 of api.mdx). Means YAML pollutes embeddings with key:value boilerplate. docforge uses Context7-style inline provenance instead: # Title 

 Source: relative/path.html 

 body. No --- YAML block.

qmd embeds chunks as `title + chunk_text` only (store.ts:1524 + llm.ts:51 formatDocForEmbedding). YAML frontmatter (summary/glossary) lives only in chunk 0; chunks 1+ get bare body. Big docs (>4K tokens, ~50 of 642 diadok) lose EN-query retrieval after chunk 0. `addContext` is display-only, NOT embedded. Fix tracked in docf-1fi: docforge post-enrich injects per-section anchor block (title + summary 1-line + top-N glossary keys) before each H2.
